description:
The release workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) does not set `NPM_CONFIG_PROVENANCE: 'true'`. This means published npm packages lack signed provenance attestation — a trust signal that npm now surfaces on the package page and that users can verify with `npm audit signatures`.

The helix repo (`../helix`) has this enabled. helixir should match.

**Fix:** Add `NPM_CONFIG_PROVENANCE: 'true'` to the `env:` block of the release job in `.github/workflows/release.yml`.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `NPM_CONFIG_PROVENANCE: 'true'` is present in the release job env block
- Workflow YAML is valid (no syntax errors)
- No other workflow steps are modified
title: chore: add NPM provenance signing to release workflow
